title: coptis trifolia subsp. groenlandica
intro: Coptis trifolia subsp. groenlandica, also known as Goldenseal, is a perennial plant native to the Arctic regions of North America and Greenland, characterized by its yellowish-green flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant contains berberine, a medicinal alkaloid with antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Goldenseal has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Goldenseal include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
